sections.title: Constraint Summary
sections.content:
| # | Constraint | Type | is_absolute | Threshold | Unit |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Aqueous Media Requirement | physical | **true** | — | — |
| 2 | Maximum Medium Temperature | physical | false | 90 | °C |
| 3 | Air Bubble / Void Fraction Limit | physical | **true** | 2 | % vol |
| 4 | Temperature-Dependent Accuracy | physical | false | 1.5 (uncompensated) | % error |
| 5 | Wetted Material Chemical Compatibility | chemical | false | — | — |
| 6 | Pipe Full Condition Required | operational | **true** | 100 | % fill |
| 7 | Minimum Flow Rate Detection | operational | false | 0.25–4.2 | l/min |
| 8 | Maximum Operating Pressure | operational | false | 10 | bar |
| 9 | Minimum Straight Pipe Run | operational | false | 5–10 | × DN |
| 10 | Life Endurance Ceiling | economic | false | 3,000 | m³ total |
| 11 | No Explosive Atmosphere Certification | regulatory | false | Zone 1/0 | ATEX zone |
| 12 | Ambient Temperature Range | environmental | false | −20 to +60 (est.) | °C ambient |

**Totals:** 12 constraints — 3 absolute, 9 conditional. Types: 4 physical, 1 chemical, 4 operational, 1 economic, 1 regulatory, 1 environmental.

---

sections.title: Detailed Constraints
sections.content:
### Physical (4)

| Field | C1: Aqueous Media Requirement |
|---|---|
| **constraint_type** | physical |
| **is_absolute** | **true** |
| **threshold_value** | — |
| **threshold_unit** | — |
| **description** | Transit-time ultrasonic measurement requires acoustic coupling between piezoelectric transducers and the fluid. Non-aqueous media (oils, gases, non-polar solvents, polymer melts) have acoustic impedance mismatches that prevent signal propagation through the sensor's current transducer-fluid interface. Water, water-glycol mixtures, and aqueous suspensions are supported. [SRC: 01_product_decomposition — "Only water and water-like media"] |
| **affected_markets_hint** | Oil & Gas processing, petrochemical refining, gas distribution, plastics extrusion, solvent-based chemical manufacturing |

| Field | C2: Maximum Medium Temperature |
|---|---|
| **constraint_type** | physical |
| **is_absolute** | **false** |
| **threshold_value** | 90 |
| **threshold_unit** | °C |
| **description** | Polymer housing and seals degrade above 90°C. Extended operation near the limit reduces service life. This is a material constraint of the current product design, not a fundamental physics limit of transit-time technology — metal-housed ultrasonic sensors exist for higher temperatures. [SRC: 01_product_decomposition — spec "Medium Temperature: +2 to 90 °C"] |
| **affected_markets_hint** | Steam systems, high-temperature industrial processes, sterilization circuits (>121°C), geothermal heating |

| Field | C3: Air Bubble / Void Fraction Limit |
|---|---|
| **constraint_type** | physical |
| **is_absolute** | **true** |
| **threshold_value** | 2 |
| **threshold_unit** | % vol |
| **description** | Air entrainment >2% by volume attenuates the ultrasonic signal and introduces measurement error. Above ~5% void fraction, signal scatter prevents any usable transit-time measurement. The sensor detects bubbles as a diagnostic event but cannot compensate for chronic high-void-fraction media. Systems with persistent air entrainment require upstream de-aerators. [SRC: 01_product_decomposition — "Air Bubble Tolerance" constraint + "Digital Anomaly Detection" feature] |
| **affected_markets_hint** | Aerated beverage processing, open-loop cooling towers, pulp & paper (high-consistency stock), fermentation processes |

| Field | C4: Temperature-Dependent Accuracy |
|---|---|
| **constraint_type** | physical |
| **is_absolute** | **false** |
| **threshold_value** | 1.5 (uncompensated); 0.2 (compensated) |
| **threshold_unit** | % error |
| **description** | Speed of sound in the medium changes with temperature. Without compensation, temperature-induced measurement error reaches ~1.5%. The integrated NTC thermistor reduces this to ~0.2% under steady-state conditions. Extreme temperature transients may temporarily exceed compensation response. Fluid type must be configured at commissioning. [SRC: 01_product_decomposition — "Temperature-Dependent Accuracy" constraint + "Integrated Temperature Sensing" feature] |
| **affected_markets_hint** | Rapid thermal cycling processes, solar thermal systems with cloud-induced transients, heat recovery with fluctuating source temperatures |

### Chemical (1)

| Field | C5: Wetted Material Chemical Compatibility |
|---|---|
| **constraint_type** | chemical |
| **is_absolute** | **false** |
| **threshold_value** | — |
| **threshold_unit** | — |
| **description** | The sensor's wetted parts (polymer housing, seals) are approved for potable water contact (KTW/WRAS) but are not rated for aggressive chemical media — concentrated acids, bases, oxidizers, or organic solvents would attack the polymer housing and seal materials. Alternative wetted materials (stainless steel, PTFE-lined, Hastelloy) are standard in the flowmeter industry but not available in the current Marquardt product line. [SRC: 01_product_decomposition — "KTW/WRAS Drinking Water Approval" feature + polymer housing material implied by temperature/pressure constraints] |
| **affected_markets_hint** | Chemical manufacturing, semiconductor wet processing, electroplating, pharmaceutical active ingredient synthesis |

### Operational (4)

| Field | C6: Pipe Full Condition Required |
|---|---|
| **constraint_type** | operational |
| **is_absolute** | **true** |
| **threshold_value** | 100 |
| **threshold_unit** | % fill |
| **description** | The pipe must be completely filled with liquid for valid measurement. Partially filled pipes cause complete signal loss — the ultrasonic pulse cannot traverse an air gap. The sensor detects empty-pipe condition as a fault state. This is inherent to inline transit-time technology; no engineering workaround exists for partially filled conduits. [SRC: 01_product_decomposition — "Pipe Full Condition" constraint] |
| **affected_markets_hint** | Open-channel irrigation, gravity-fed drainage, sewer systems, partially filled process drains |

| Field | C7: Minimum Flow Rate Detection |
|---|---|
| **constraint_type** | operational |
| **is_absolute** | **false** |
| **threshold_value** | 0.25–4.2 |
| **threshold_unit** | l/min |
| **description** | Signal-to-noise ratio degrades at very low flow rates — the transit-time difference becomes indistinguishable from noise. The lower detection limit varies by pipe diameter: ~0.25 l/min (DN14), ~4.2 l/min (DN20/DN25). Applications requiring micro-flow measurement below these thresholds cannot use this sensor without a smaller-bore variant. [SRC: 01_product_decomposition — spec "Flow Range" per variant] |
| **affected_markets_hint** | Laboratory dosing, medical infusion, micro-dispensing, semiconductor ultra-pure water (low-flow branches) |

| Field | C8: Maximum Operating Pressure |
|---|---|
| **constraint_type** | operational |
| **is_absolute** | **false** |
| **threshold_value** | 10 |
| **threshold_unit** | bar |
| **description** | Housing structural integrity rated to 10 bar continuous. Burst pressure is 9 bar (low-pressure variant) or 42 bar (high-pressure variant). Exceeding the rating risks seal failure and leakage. Higher-pressure variants or reinforced housings are a standard design path in the flowmeter industry. [SRC: 01_product_decomposition — spec "Pressure Range: 0–10 bar", "Burst Pressure: 9/42 bar"] |
| **affected_markets_hint** | High-pressure water-jet cutting, boiler feed water (>10 bar), reverse osmosis systems, fire suppression mains |

| Field | C9: Minimum Straight Pipe Run |
|---|---|
| **constraint_type** | operational |
| **is_absolute** | **false** |
| **threshold_value** | 5–10 |
| **threshold_unit** | × DN |
| **description** | Turbulent or asymmetric flow profiles caused by elbows, valves, or tees upstream degrade measurement accuracy. A minimum of 5–10 pipe diameters of undisturbed straight run is recommended upstream of the sensor. Flow conditioners can reduce this requirement. [SRC: 01_product_decomposition — "Minimum Straight Pipe Run" constraint; 02_functional_promise — "Straight pipe run (upstream)" complement] |
| **affected_markets_hint** | Compact appliances (heat pumps, combi boilers), retrofits in constrained piping, skid-mounted process equipment |

### Economic (1)

| Field | C10: Life Endurance Ceiling |
|---|---|
| **constraint_type** | economic |
| **is_absolute** | **false** |
| **threshold_value** | 3,000 |
| **threshold_unit** | m³ total throughput |
| **description** | Total throughput endurance rated at 3,000 m³. At maximum flow (46.6 l/min DN20), this equates to ~1,073 hours (~45 days continuous). High-flow industrial applications running 24/7 would exhaust sensor life within weeks, requiring frequent replacement. For typical residential/commercial HVAC duty cycles (intermittent, lower flow), this equates to years of service. Replacement cost becomes uneconomical in high-throughput continuous-process applications. [SRC: 01_product_decomposition — spec "Life Endurance: 3,000 m³"] |
| **affected_markets_hint** | Municipal water metering (high throughput), industrial process water loops (24/7), district heating mains |

### Regulatory (1)

| Field | C11: No Explosive Atmosphere Certification |
|---|---|
| **constraint_type** | regulatory |
| **is_absolute** | **false** |
| **threshold_value** | Zone 1/0 |
| **threshold_unit** | ATEX zone |
| **description** | The current product configuration has no ATEX/IECEx certification for deployment in explosive atmospheres. No hazardous area approval is mentioned in the product specifications or feature set. A certification path exists via intrinsically safe (Ex-ia) barrier modules or Ex-d/Ex-e enclosure designs — standard practice in the flowmeter industry. [SRC: 01_product_decomposition — absence of any ATEX/Ex feature; KTW/WRAS is the only certification mentioned] |
| **affected_markets_hint** | Oil refining, petrochemical processing, solvent handling, grain/dust handling facilities, paint manufacturing |

### Environmental (1)

| Field | C12: Ambient Temperature Range |
|---|---|
| **constraint_type** | environmental |
| **is_absolute** | **false** |
| **threshold_value** | −20 to +60 (est.) |
| **threshold_unit** | °C ambient |
| **description** | The sensor electronics and polymer housing are designed for indoor / protected installation environments typical of HVAC and appliance OEM applications. No IP rating beyond basic splash protection is evident from the specifications. Extreme ambient conditions — sub-zero outdoor installations, desert environments, or locations near furnaces — may exceed the electronics' operating range. Heated or cooled enclosures are a standard mitigation. The RAST 2.5 connector is an appliance-grade connector, not an industrial-grade sealed connection. [SRC: 01_product_decomposition — spec "Supply Voltage: 5/6–30 V DC", "Connector: RAST 2.5"; 02_functional_promise — BOM Level L5 Component, parent subsystem "hydronic circuit, cooling loop"] |
| **affected_markets_hint** | Outdoor water metering (arctic/desert climates), rooftop HVAC in extreme environments, agricultural irrigation in open fields |

---

sections.title: Constraint Type Coverage
sections.content:
| Type | Count | Absolute | Conditional |
|---|---|---|---|
| physical | 4 | 2 (C1, C3) | 2 (C2, C4) |
| chemical | 1 | 0 | 1 (C5) |
| operational | 4 | 1 (C6) | 3 (C7, C8, C9) |
| economic | 1 | 0 | 1 (C10) |
| regulatory | 1 | 0 | 1 (C11) |
| environmental | 1 | 0 | 1 (C12) |
| **Total** | **12** | **3** | **9** |

---

sections.title: Absolute vs Conditional Interpretation
sections.content:
| Classification | Meaning | Constraints |
|---|---|---|
| **is_absolute = true** | Fundamental physical impossibility. No redesign, money, or time overcomes it. The constraint IS the physics. | C1 (non-aqueous media), C3 (high void fraction), C6 (partially filled pipe) |
| **is_absolute = false** | Barrier exists but known engineering solutions, design modifications, or certifications can overcome it. | C2, C4, C5, C7, C8, C9, C10, C11, C12 |

---

sections.title: Downstream Use
sections.content:
- **Step 08 (Constraint-Market Compatibility):** Each constraint will be assessed against each candidate market from Market Discovery. Markets with any knockout (absolute constraint exceeded in ALL normal operating conditions) are eliminated.
- **Fit Assessment:** Mitigable constraints carry cost/time penalties into the Constraint Readiness (CR) dimension of market fit scores.
- **Market Prioritization:** Eliminated markets are excluded entirely; mitigable costs factor into final ranking.

---

sections.title: QA Checklist
sections.content:
| # | Check | Result |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | All 6 constraint types represented | PASS — 4 physical, 1 chemical, 4 operational, 1 economic, 1 regulatory, 1 environmental |
| 2 | is_absolute only for physics impossibilities | PASS — 3 absolute (media, void fraction, pipe-full); all are fundamental measurement principle limits |
| 3 | Threshold values are specific numbers with units | PASS — all thresholds have numeric values and units where applicable |
| 4 | affected_markets_hint present (2–5 per constraint) | PASS — all constraints have 3–5 hints |
| 5 | No certifiable regulation marked as absolute | PASS — ATEX = is_absolute: false |
| 6 | No cost barrier marked as absolute | PASS — Life Endurance = is_absolute: false |
| 7 | All Step 01 constraints enriched | PASS — all 8 original constraints present + 4 new (C5, C10, C11, C12) |
| 8 | Source tags on factual claims | PASS — [SRC] tags reference upstream files |

---

sections.title: Sources
sections.content:
- 01_product_decomposition.md — constraints, specifications, features, technology classification
- 02_functional_promise.md — complements, BOM position, functional promise scope

rows.C1: Aqueous Media Requirement: Transit-time ultrasonic measurement requires acoustic coupling between piezoelectric transducers and the fluid. Non-aqueous media (oils, gases, non-polar solvents, polymer melts) have acoustic impedance mismatches that prevent signal propagation through the sensor's current transducer-fluid interface. Water, water-glycol mixtures, and aqueous suspensions are supported. [SRC: 01_product_decomposition — "Only water and water-like media"]
Oil & Gas processing, petrochemical refining, gas distribution, plastics extrusion, solvent-based chemical manufacturing

rows.C2: Maximum Medium Temperature: Polymer housing and seals degrade above 90°C. Extended operation near the limit reduces service life. This is a material constraint of the current product design, not a fundamental physics limit of transit-time technology — metal-housed ultrasonic sensors exist for higher temperatures. [SRC: 01_product_decomposition — spec "Medium Temperature: +2 to 90 °C"]
Steam systems, high-temperature industrial processes, sterilization circuits (>121°C), geothermal heating

headers: C3: Air Bubble / Void Fraction Limit

rows.C3: Air Bubble / Void Fraction Limit: Air entrainment >2% by volume attenuates the ultrasonic signal and introduces measurement error. Above ~5% void fraction, signal scatter prevents any usable transit-time measurement. The sensor detects bubbles as a diagnostic event but cannot compensate for chronic high-void-fraction media. Systems with persistent air entrainment require upstream de-aerators. [SRC: 01_product_decomposition — "Air Bubble Tolerance" constraint + "Digital Anomaly Detection" feature]
Aerated beverage processing, open-loop cooling towers, pulp & paper (high-consistency stock), fermentation processes

rows.C4: Temperature-Dependent Accuracy: Speed of sound in the medium changes with temperature. Without compensation, temperature-induced measurement error reaches ~1.5%. The integrated NTC thermistor reduces this to ~0.2% under steady-state conditions. Extreme temperature transients may temporarily exceed compensation response. Fluid type must be configured at commissioning. [SRC: 01_product_decomposition — "Temperature-Dependent Accuracy" constraint + "Integrated Temperature Sensing" feature]
Rapid thermal cycling processes, solar thermal systems with cloud-induced transients, heat recovery with fluctuating source temperatures

rows.C5: Wetted Material Chemical Compatibili: The sensor's wetted parts (polymer housing, seals) are approved for potable water contact (KTW/WRAS) but are not rated for aggressive chemical media — concentrated acids, bases, oxidizers, or organic solvents would attack the polymer housing and seal materials. Alternative wetted materials (stainless steel, PTFE-lined, Hastelloy) are standard in the flowmeter industry but not available in the current Marquardt product line. [SRC: 01_product_decomposition — "KTW/WRAS Drinking Water Approval" feature + polymer housing material implied by temperature/pressure constraints]
Chemical manufacturing, semiconductor wet processing, electroplating, pharmaceutical active ingredient synthesis

rows.C6: Pipe Full Condition Required: The pipe must be completely filled with liquid for valid measurement. Partially filled pipes cause complete signal loss — the ultrasonic pulse cannot traverse an air gap. The sensor detects empty-pipe condition as a fault state. This is inherent to inline transit-time technology; no engineering workaround exists for partially filled conduits. [SRC: 01_product_decomposition — "Pipe Full Condition" constraint]
Open-channel irrigation, gravity-fed drainage, sewer systems, partially filled process drains

rows.C7: Minimum Flow Rate Detection: Signal-to-noise ratio degrades at very low flow rates — the transit-time difference becomes indistinguishable from noise. The lower detection limit varies by pipe diameter: ~0.25 l/min (DN14), ~4.2 l/min (DN20/DN25). Applications requiring micro-flow measurement below these thresholds cannot use this sensor without a smaller-bore variant. [SRC: 01_product_decomposition — spec "Flow Range" per variant]
Laboratory dosing, medical infusion, micro-dispensing, semiconductor ultra-pure water (low-flow branches)

rows.C8: Maximum Operating Pressure: Housing structural integrity rated to 10 bar continuous. Burst pressure is 9 bar (low-pressure variant) or 42 bar (high-pressure variant). Exceeding the rating risks seal failure and leakage. Higher-pressure variants or reinforced housings are a standard design path in the flowmeter industry. [SRC: 01_product_decomposition — spec "Pressure Range: 0–10 bar", "Burst Pressure: 9/42 bar"]
High-pressure water-jet cutting, boiler feed water (>10 bar), reverse osmosis systems, fire suppression mains

rows.C9: Minimum Straight Pipe Run: Turbulent or asymmetric flow profiles caused by elbows, valves, or tees upstream degrade measurement accuracy. A minimum of 5–10 pipe diameters of undisturbed straight run is recommended upstream of the sensor. Flow conditioners can reduce this requirement. [SRC: 01_product_decomposition — "Minimum Straight Pipe Run" constraint; 02_functional_promise — "Straight pipe run (upstream)" complement]
Compact appliances (heat pumps, combi boilers), retrofits in constrained piping, skid-mounted process equipment

rows.C10: Life Endurance Ceiling: Total throughput endurance rated at 3,000 m³. At maximum flow (46.6 l/min DN20), this equates to ~1,073 hours (~45 days continuous). High-flow industrial applications running 24/7 would exhaust sensor life within weeks, requiring frequent replacement. For typical residential/commercial HVAC duty cycles (intermittent, lower flow), this equates to years of service. Replacement cost becomes uneconomical in high-throughput continuous-process applications. [SRC: 01_product_decomposition — spec "Life Endurance: 3,000 m³"]
Municipal water metering (high throughput), industrial process water loops (24/7), district heating mains

rows.C11: No Explosive Atmosphere Certificati: The current product configuration has no ATEX/IECEx certification for deployment in explosive atmospheres. No hazardous area approval is mentioned in the product specifications or feature set. A certification path exists via intrinsically safe (Ex-ia) barrier modules or Ex-d/Ex-e enclosure designs — standard practice in the flowmeter industry. [SRC: 01_product_decomposition — absence of any ATEX/Ex feature; KTW/WRAS is the only certification mentioned]
Oil refining, petrochemical processing, solvent handling, grain/dust handling facilities, paint manufacturing

rows.C12: Ambient Temperature Range: The sensor electronics and polymer housing are designed for indoor / protected installation environments typical of HVAC and appliance OEM applications. No IP rating beyond basic splash protection is evident from the specifications. Extreme ambient conditions — sub-zero outdoor installations, desert environments, or locations near furnaces — may exceed the electronics' operating range. Heated or cooled enclosures are a standard mitigation. The RAST 2.5 connector is an appliance-grade connector, not an industrial-grade sealed connection. [SRC: 01_product_decomposition — spec "Supply Voltage: 5/6–30 V DC", "Connector: RAST 2.5"; 02_functional_promise — BOM Level L5 Component, parent subsystem "hydronic circuit, cooling loop"]
Outdoor water metering (arctic/desert climates), rooftop HVAC in extreme environments, agricultural irrigation in open fields

rows.Meaning: Fundamental physical impossibility. No redesign, money, or time overcomes it. The constraint IS the physics.
rows.Constraints: C1 (non-aqueous media), C3 (high void fraction), C6 (partially filled pipe)

rows.Meaning: Barrier exists but known engineering solutions, design modifications, or certifications can overcome it.

rows.Result: PASS — 4 physical, 1 chemical, 4 operational, 1 economic, 1 regulatory, 1 environmental
PASS — 3 absolute (media, void fraction, pipe-full); all are fundamental measurement principle limits

rows.Check: Threshold values are specific numbers with units
rows.Result: PASS — all thresholds have numeric values and units where applicable

rows.Check: No certifiable regulation marked as absolute
No cost barrier marked as absolute

rows.Result: PASS — all 8 original constraints present + 4 new (C5, C10, C11, C12)

constraints.name: Aqueous Media Requirement
constraints.description: Transit-time ultrasonic measurement requires acoustic coupling between piezoelectric transducers and the fluid. Non-aqueous media (oils, gases, non-polar solvents, polymer melts) have acoustic impedance mismatches that prevent signal propagation. Water, water-glycol mixtures, and aqueous suspensions are supported.

constraints.name: Maximum Medium Temperature
constraints.description: Polymer housing and seals degrade above 90°C. Extended operation near limit reduces service life. Material constraint of the current product design, not a fundamental limit of transit-time technology.

constraints.name: Air Bubble / Void Fraction Limit
constraints.description: Air entrainment >2% by volume attenuates ultrasonic signal. Above ~5% void fraction, signal scatter prevents any usable transit-time measurement. Chronic high-void-fraction media require upstream de-aerators.

constraints.name: Temperature-Dependent Accuracy
constraints.description: Speed of sound changes with temperature. Uncompensated error ~1.5%. Built-in NTC compensation reduces to ~0.2% steady-state. Extreme transients may temporarily exceed compensation response.

constraints.name: Wetted Material Chemical Compatibility
constraints.description: Wetted parts (polymer housing, seals) approved for potable water contact (KTW/WRAS) but not rated for aggressive chemicals — concentrated acids, bases, oxidizers, or organic solvents would attack the housing. Alternative wetted materials are standard in the flowmeter industry but not in the current Marquardt product line.

constraints.name: Pipe Full Condition Required
constraints.description: Pipe must be completely filled with liquid. Partially filled pipes cause complete signal loss — ultrasonic pulse cannot traverse an air gap. Inherent to inline transit-time technology.

constraints.name: Minimum Flow Rate Detection
constraints.description: SNR degrades at very low flow rates. Lower detection limit varies by pipe diameter: ~0.25 l/min (DN14), ~4.2 l/min (DN20/DN25).

constraints.name: Maximum Operating Pressure
constraints.description: Housing rated to 10 bar continuous. Burst pressure 9 bar (low-pressure variant) or 42 bar (high-pressure variant). Higher-pressure variants are a standard design path.

constraints.name: Minimum Straight Pipe Run
constraints.description: Turbulent or asymmetric flow profiles from upstream disturbances degrade accuracy. 5-10 pipe diameters of straight run recommended upstream. Flow conditioners can reduce this requirement.

constraints.name: Life Endurance Ceiling
constraints.description: Total throughput endurance rated at 3,000 m³. At max flow (46.6 l/min DN20), this equates to ~45 days continuous. High-flow 24/7 applications exhaust sensor life quickly, making replacement cost uneconomical.

constraints.name: No Explosive Atmosphere Certification
constraints.description: No ATEX/IECEx certification for explosive atmospheres. Certification path exists via intrinsically safe (Ex-ia) barrier modules or Ex-d/Ex-e enclosure designs.

constraints.name: Ambient Temperature Range
constraints.description: Electronics and polymer housing designed for indoor/protected installation. RAST 2.5 connector is appliance-grade. Extreme ambient conditions may exceed electronics operating range. Heated/cooled enclosures are a standard mitigation.
